notes: Antigonus was a powerful successor (Diadochi) of Alexander the Great who established a dynasty and took the title of king.

docs.title: Life of Cleomenes
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … to come to terms with Antigonus, a brave warrior, and a man of action, to wait an effeminate king’s leisure, till he should lay aside his timbrel and end his dance, and then kill him.

docs.title: Life of Demetrius
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: But he was of a violent and haughty spirit; and the insulting words as well as actions in which he allowed himself could not be borne by young and powerful princes, and provoked them into combining against him.

docs.title: Life of Pelopidas
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … thou reckon me?” intimating that a brave and experienced commander is to be highly valued, one of the first duties of whose office indeed it is to save him on whose safety depends that of others.
